analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 8 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: AES wrapper exposes key_in/key_big and inter_state through software-visible register read data without an authorization/debug/lifecycle guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose / AES key material key_in/key_big and intermediate state inter_state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible AXI/APB/register requester routed to AES through ariane_peripherals aes port and external_bus_io Operation: read/expose Object: AES key material key_in/key_big and intermediate state inter_state Expected guard: Key and AES intermediate-state readback should be blocked or mediated by an explicit authorization/debug/lifecycle guard; normal software-readable registers should not expose key_in/key_big or inter_state unless authorized. Observed behavior: external_bus_io.ready is tied high and error low; combinational rdata mux decodes only external_bus_io.addr[6:2] and returns key_big slices at key addresses and inter_state slices at intermediate-state addresses. No privilege, debug, lifecycle, lock, or authorization condition is visible in aes_wrapper. Path: tb/ariane_peripherals.sv AXI aes port -> axi2apb_64_32 -> apb_to_reg i_apb_to_reg_aes -> reg_bus_aes -> aes_wrapper.external_bus_io -> external_bus_io.rdata Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Any software-visible requester that can reach AES MMIO can read AES key material and internal AES intermediate state.

findings.summary: AES control/plaintext/state registers are writable via external_bus_io.write with only address decode and no visible privilege/lock/lifecycle or busy-state guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure/trigger / AES control start, plaintext p_c, and state registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible AXI/APB/register requester via external_bus_io Operation: write/configure/trigger Object: AES control start, plaintext p_c, and state registers Expected guard: Configuration of AES plaintext/control/state registers should be restricted to intended bus writes and, if state is protected, mediated by privilege, lock, or lifecycle controls; writes should not occur from reads or unqualified bus activity. Observed behavior: On reset start/p_c/state clear. Otherwise, if external_bus_io.write is asserted, aes_wrapper case-decodes addr[6:2] and writes start, p_c[3:0], or state[3:0]. No local psel/penable, privilege, lifecycle, lock, ct_valid, or busy guard is visible. Path: AXI aes -> axi2apb_64_32 -> apb_to_reg i_apb_to_reg_aes -> reg_bus_aes.write/wdata/addr -> aes_wrapper start/p_c/state -> aes_192_sed inputs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Any requester reaching AES MMIO can trigger AES and alter plaintext/state inputs without an AES-local authorization guard.

findings.summary: Visible harness wires SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or SimDTM DMI traffic into dm_top, and dm_top emits an AXI master request connected to slave[1] of the SoC fabric. test_en is tied low. dmi_jtag receives jtag_key, but dmi_jtag/dm_top internals and denial/authentication behavior are not included in the provided evidence.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/trigger via debug / SoC memory/peripheral address space, including bootrom, rom2, AES, and security-sensitive MMIO / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/DMI debug requester Operation: read/write/trigger via debug Object: SoC memory/peripheral address space, including bootrom, rom2, AES, and security-sensitive MMIO Expected guard: JTAG/DMI debug accesses that can issue memory/peripheral operations should be gated by debug authentication/authorization or lifecycle/test-mode policy before reaching protected address ranges. Observed behavior: Visible harness wires SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or SimDTM DMI traffic into dm_top, and dm_top emits an AXI master request connected to slave[1] of the SoC fabric. test_en is tied low. dmi_jtag receives jtag_key, but dmi_jtag/dm_top internals and denial/authentication behavior are not included in the provided evidence. Path: SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or SimDTM -> debug_req/debug_resp DMI mux -> dm_top -> dm_axi_m_req -> axi_master_connect i_axi_master_dm -> slave[1] -> axi_node_intf_wrap -> memory/peripheral fabric Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Debug-to-fabric reachability is present; whether protected accesses are authorized or denied cannot be confirmed from visible scope.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dmi_jtag and dm_top internals; exact authentication comparison and access denial behavior.

findings.summary: jtag_key is exported from ROM2-derived key registers and fed to dmi_jtag, while ROM2 is writable via AXI; no visible authentication comparison semantics are available in inspected RTL.
findings.vulnerability_category: expose/unlock / jtag_key debug/unlock token / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/debug interface and any external/software observer of jtag_key Operation: expose/unlock Object: jtag_key debug/unlock token Expected guard: Any JTAG unlock key or debug authorization token should not be exposed to unauthorized software-visible or external outputs, and use of the key should be tied to an authentication comparison rather than a mere signal presence. Observed behavior: ariane_peripherals assigns output jtag_key = key_reg_out[1][31:0]. key_reg_out is rom2.secure_reg. rom2 loads constants on reset but also writes secure_reg on req_i && we_i. jtag_key is connected at testharness to dmi_jtag. No comparison/guard is visible in provided files. Path: rom2 secure_reg[1] -> key_reg_out -> ariane_peripherals.jtag_key output -> ariane_testharness.jtag_key -> dmi_jtag.jtag_key Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A debug/unlock token is derived from writable key storage and exported across module boundary; if used as authorization, it may be observable or alterable by bus access to ROM2.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dmi_jtag internal comparison/use semantics are not visible.

findings.summary: ROM2 is not immutable: bus writes update secure_reg containing AES/JTAG/access-control key material; ROM2 readback also exposes secure_reg contents.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/expose / rom2 memory contents, secure_reg key storage, jtag_key/access_ctrl derived values, and pkey_loc_o/key-location related data / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB/AXI requester reaching rom2 Operation: read/write/expose Object: rom2 memory contents, secure_reg key storage, jtag_key/access_ctrl derived values, and pkey_loc_o/key-location related data Expected guard: ROM-like protected contents should ignore or reject writes and should only expose intended read data for authorized address ranges; any pkey/key-location output should be mediated if sensitive. Observed behavior: rom2 writes secure_reg[addr] <= wdata_i when req_i && we_i. rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q]. ariane_peripherals connects rom2 through axi2mem, exports secure_reg as key_reg_out, assigns jtag_key and access_ctrl_reg from it. pkey_loc_o exists and is connected to a packet/peripheral-key-table area, but its exact sensitivity/use is not fully shown. Path: AXI rom2_fuse -> axi2mem i_axi2rom2 -> rom2 req/we/addr/wdata/rdata -> secure_reg/key_reg_out -> jtag_key/access_ctrl_reg Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Bus requesters that can reach ROM2 can alter AES/JTAG keys and access-control policy values and can read stored key material; this can undermine both secrets and fabric permission policy.

findings.summary: Boot ROM memory is writable when req_i && we_i, contrary to expected ROM immutability.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/read / bootrom memory contents and boot code read data / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI/requester reaching bootrom Operation: write/read Object: bootrom memory contents and boot code read data Expected guard: Boot ROM should be immutable from bus writes and read access should be limited to intended address range; write-enable inputs should not alter ROM contents or bypass boot policy. Observed behavior: bootrom has an always_ff block that on req_i and we_i writes mem[addr_i index] <= wdata_i. Reads update raddr_q only when !we_i and rdata_o returns mem[raddr_q] if in range else zero. In testharness, axi2mem drives rom_req/rom_we/rom_addr/rom_wdata from master[ROM] to bootrom. Path: AXI/testharness bus master[ariane_soc::ROM] -> axi2mem i_axi2rom -> bootrom req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i/rdata_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Any requester able to issue writes to the ROM address region can modify boot code/data, violating boot ROM immutability and potentially boot trust assumptions.

findings.summary: APB-visible peripherals, including AES, are bridged from AXI to apb_to_reg without visible APB-level privilege/security checks; AES register block then exposes secrets and accepts writes based on address/write only.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/configure / APB-visible peripheral registers including AES and interrupt/control registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible bus requester through APB bridge Operation: read/write/configure Object: APB-visible peripheral registers including AES and interrupt/control registers Expected guard: APB peripheral selection and write/read enables should include correct address decode and, for protected peripherals such as AES and interrupt control, expected privilege or policy checks before register access. Observed behavior: ariane_peripherals instantiates axi2apb_64_32 and apb_to_reg for PLIC, PKT, AES, UART-like blocks. AES bridge passes AWPROT/ARPROT into axi2apb but no explicit privilege/policy check is visible at APB-to-reg or aes_wrapper; aes_wrapper uses external_bus_io.write and addr only. test_en_i on bridges is tied 1'b0. Path: AXI peripheral ports -> axi2apb_64_32 -> APB psel/pwrite/penable/paddr -> apb_to_reg -> REG_BUS -> peripheral register blocks such as aes_wrapper Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Protected APB registers can be accessed by any requester admitted by upstream fabric; APB conversion does not itself preserve/enforce privilege policy for AES secret read/write behavior.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full axi2apb/apb_to_reg internals are not provided; upstream fabric may provide region-level policy, but no APB-level protected-register mediation is visible.

findings.summary: Debug ndmreset resets peripherals including AES, but ROM2 reloads key material and AES wrapper leaves key_in/key_big externally supplied and readable through AES rdata after reset; reset does not eliminate secret readback risk.
findings.vulnerability_category: reset/expose / AES key/intermediate/ciphertext/status state and debug/security control state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug/JTAG reset requester and software bus requester Operation: reset/expose Object: AES key/intermediate/ciphertext/status state and debug/security control state Expected guard: Debug-triggered or non-debug resets should clear or preserve security-sensitive state according to policy and should not leave stale key/intermediate/output data externally readable after reset or mode transition. Observed behavior: ndmreset from dm_top drives rstgen to create ndmreset_n used by bootrom bridge, peripherals, and core. aes_wrapper resets start/p_c/state but key_big is continuous key_in and inter_state/ct are AES outputs; the AES read mux remains able to return key_big and inter_state. ariane_peripherals reset ndmreset_n resets rom2, causing secure_reg to reload constant keys, which feed AES key_in via key_reg_out[0]. Path: dm_top.ndmreset_o -> rstgen -> ndmreset_n -> ariane_peripherals/aes_wrapper/rom2; rom2 secure_reg -> key_reg_out[0] -> aes_wrapper.key_in/key_big -> external_bus_io.rdata Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A debug reset can return the system to a state where key material is reloaded and still software-readable via AES MMIO; reset behavior does not mitigate secret exposure and may refresh exposed key values.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
